raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a pump from XXXX XXXX XXXX listed as an open box item. It listed pump only and rated XXXX star. Their star-rating system states that a XXXX item XXXX have cosmetic blemishes or missing packaging but will still include all required parts to operate. However, the pump I received was missing essential components and was not functional. 

I filed a dispute with Citi to contest the charge, explaining that the merchant misrepresented the item. Citi investigated and sent me a letter with their decision. 

Citi reapplied the charge to my account even though the merchant did not deliver a product that matched its description. An incomplete pump that can not function is not what was advertised. This makes the charge invalid, and Citis resolution does not align with the facts. 

I am requesting that Citi reverse the charge in full, as the merchant did not provide the product as described. 

Date purchased XX/XX/year> Amount XXXX Date received XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX contacted about missing parts XX/XX/year> The pump is missing two parts to make it operational. Without these, the pump is 100 % non-operational. 

A controller valued at {$120.00} An energy supply pack valued {$120.00}
